prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `319f2597`.
codex_response:
The rule is: locate the unique `2x2` black (`0`) block, then black out its two rows and two columns across the whole grid, while preserving any red `2` cells already lying on that cross. I used the `opus46` hint as the starting hypothesis and added [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_319f2597/notes.md) because the `sonnet45` summary was incomplete about that red-cell preservation applying to the full cross, not just the marker rows.

The generator matches the task family by producing dense random `20x20` fields over colors `1..9`, inserting a single central-ish `2x2` zero marker, and re-rolling until the eventual cross contains several protected `2`s so the output looks like the official examples. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: official verifier `4/4`, generated verifier `1000/1000`, and the built preview/task artifacts were produced successfully.
